title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has issued a response to a report on Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Public Standards, outlining its plans to address the recommendations made by the committee. The government's response aims to ensure that AI is developed and used in ways that benefit society, while also mitigating potential risks and consequences.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report assesses the impact of using copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It explores the potential effects of this practice on copyright law and rights holders.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education's policy on generative AI in education outlines the government's stance on the use of artificial intelligence in educational settings. The policy is likely intended to provide guidance on the safe and effective use of AI in teaching, learning, and assessment.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This document outlines a plan for the Department for Transport (DfT) to work with the transport industry to safely implement and benefit from the use of artificial intelligence. The plan aims to balance the opportunities of AI with the potential risks in the transport sector.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This policy report discusses the use of copyrighted works in creating artificial intelligence systems. It is likely a progress update under a specific section of a data access law.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to recommendations from the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) about how to regulate artificial intelligence used in medical devices. This response aims to ensure AI medical devices meet necessary safety and quality standards.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document explains Ofqual's plan for overseeing the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications. It responds to government requests to address AI's impact on the qualifications sector.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: This document is the government's response to the House of Lords Select Committee's report on Artificial Intelligence, which aims to address concerns and issues raised about AI use in the UK. The government outlines its actions and strategies to address these concerns and ensure safe and beneficial use of AI in the country.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The AI and Public Standards Committee will explore the intersection of artificial intelligence and public standards, analyzing their relationship and implications. The Committee's review will likely address questions and concerns about AI's impact on society and the standards to ensure its safe and responsible use.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has made a submission to an inquiry about the governance of Artificial Intelligence. The submission is likely focused on how the UK should regulate and manage AI to ensure it serves the public good.
